close_reason: Style preference: match vs if-let-else for 2-arm dispatch is subjective
description:
cargo clippy -p secretx-aws-sm -- -W clippy::pedantic flags one warning:

    warning: you seem to be trying to use 'match' for destructuring a single pattern. Consider using 'if let'
       --> secretx-aws-sm/src/lib.rs:225:9
        |
    225 | /         match std::str::from_utf8(bytes) {
    226 | |             Ok(s) => { ... }
    ...   |
    249 | |         }

Defendable to keep as match:

1. Both arms have substantive bodies (~10 lines each), not 'do something / do nothing'. clippy::single_match_else is most useful when one arm is empty or trivial.

2. The Err arm carries a multi-line ZEROIZATION GAP comment that's the most important documentation in the function. Burying it in the 'else' branch of an if-let-else costs reader attention.

3. match makes the dispatch (UTF-8 → secret_string vs binary → secret_binary) symmetrical and explicit. The two SDK builder chains are visually parallel under match arms; if-let-else makes them asymmetric.

Recommendation: keep as match, add #[allow(clippy::single_match_else)] with a one-line comment, OR leave the warning unaddressed (it's a pedantic lint, not a default lint).

If the workspace runs clippy --pedantic in CI: add the allow.
If not: leave it and close this issue as 'won't fix / opinion'.

LABEL: opinion. This is bikeshedding-adjacent; flagged only because the user asked for a Rust idiom pass.
title: secretx-aws-sm: clippy::pedantic single_match_else on put() UTF-8 dispatch — keep as match
